summary: Systems architecture review accepted FOUNDATION-004. The client shell uses a disposable ClientReadModelSnapshot projection, React holds no authoritative WorldState, map-renderer consumes read-model snapshots and can rebuild its scene shell from them, and the package/dependency shape remains within the F004 scope and frozen React/Pixi/Vite/Vitest stack choices.

tests_run.result: Confirmed branch chore/foundation-004-client-shell. Source changes are in F004 client/package paths plus lead-owned task/thread/message metadata; no accepted ADR or PROJECT_STATUS.md changes were present.
Vitest completed successfully for apps/web: 1 test file passed, 3 tests passed.
Architecture boundaries passed; self-test fixture produced 1 expected violation.
Command test:e2e not found. This is accepted as a documented deferral because FOUNDATION-005 is the active test-baseline task that owns Playwright setup and has this command in its required tests.
Prettier format check, ESLint, recursive workspace typecheck, and architecture:check all passed.

tests_run.command: rg -n "WorldState|world|sim-core|@monsoon/sim-core|useState|useReducer|createContext|redux|DisplayObject|PIXI|pixi|Date\.now|Math\.random|any| as unknown as |TODO|FIXME|skip\(" apps/web packages/client-core packages/map-renderer packages/ui
tests_run.result: Manual scan found no WorldState storage, Redux/global authority store, React-held Pixi DisplayObject state, nondeterministic APIs, banned any/double assertions, or skipped tests in the F004 client packages. Matches were limited to the existing F003 worker-compatible adapter, React useState holding the disposable snapshot, Pixi package naming/dependency, and test assertion text.

decisions: ACCEPT: React WebClientShell stores only the projected ClientReadModelSnapshot returned by client-core and passes it to a view component; it does not store authoritative WorldState or domain mutation authority.

ACCEPT: packages/client-core depends only on @monsoon/protocol, models read-model snapshots/deltas as serializable projection state, and can replace/project snapshots without retaining simulation internals.

ACCEPT: packages/map-renderer depends on @monsoon/client-core, builds a MapRenderPlan from ClientMapReadModelSnapshot, and its scene shell clears/destroys child nodes before rebuilding from the current read model.
ACCEPT: packages/ui consumes ClientReadModelSnapshot as view props only; it does not call Worker/simulation APIs, own map scene objects, or become an authority layer.
ACCEPT: package.json and pnpm-lock.yaml updates are inside the reconciled F004 scope and use frozen stack choices from docs/03: React 19.2, PixiJS 8.19, Vite 8, and Vitest.

ACCEPT: the missing pnpm test:e2e --project=chromium command is not a blocker for FOUNDATION-004 because FOUNDATION-005 explicitly owns the Vitest/fast-check/Storybook/Playwright baseline and requires that command.
risks: apps/web still consumes the FOUNDATION-003 Worker-compatible hello adapter, which dynamically imports sim-core rather than proving a real Chrome Dedicated Worker bootstrap. This remains acceptable for F004 because React only receives the DTO-derived read model, but the lead should keep the real Worker boundary/contract coverage on the upcoming client or test-baseline path before gameplay state exists.

map-renderer declares PixiJS and provides an adapter-oriented scene shell, but the current F004 implementation does not instantiate a real browser Pixi Application or run visual/interaction smoke coverage. That belongs with the Playwright/renderer baseline after FOUNDATION-005.

The architecture checker passed, but it currently covers sim-core purity and strict TS baseline more than full outward dependency direction; manual review found the F004 package imports acceptable, and future checker expansion should cover app-to-sim and UI/map boundaries explicitly.
requested_action: Record systems architecture acceptance for FOUNDATION-004, keep the e2e/real Worker/Pixi smoke deferrals attached to FOUNDATION-005 or the next client test-baseline task, and route the next lead-owned workflow step.
